kn-151: Ramu woke up early and walked to the small shop near his house.
He greeted the shopkeeper and asked for one hot coffee.
The shopkeeper asked: do you want sugar?
Ramu said: yes, a little sugar, and a little milk.
He sat on a chair and watched the morning wake up around him.
The coffee was very good. Ramu smiled and said thank you.
kn-152: Priya stood at the road and waved at an auto. The driver stopped.
She greeted him and said she needed to go to Majestic.
The driver asked how far it was. She replied: it's nearby, about 3 kilometers.
He said: 100 rupees. She said: that's too much! Give it for 50.
They agreed on 70 rupees. She got in and said: go straight.
Near Majestic she said: stop here. She paid and said thank you.
kn-153: Meera went to the market on Saturday morning.
First she bought vegetables. 2 kilos of potatoes and 1 kilo of onions.
The vegetable seller asked: want tomatoes? They're very fresh! She said: yes, give me half a kilo.
Then she went to the fruit shop. She bought 3 bananas and 2 mangoes.
How much total? The shopkeeper said: 120 rupees.
Meera said okay and paid. She carried her bag home, happy with her fresh food.
kn-154: Kiran's day started early. Yesterday he got up at 6 o'clock. He woke up in the morning.
Today he is busy. He is working. He is at the office.
In the afternoon, he ate lunch. Rice and rasam.
In the evening, he comes home. He watches TV.
Tomorrow he will go to Mysore. He will meet his friend.
Kiran is happy. Today is for work, tomorrow is for travel!
kn-155: Ananya visited her colleague Lakshmi's house for the first time.
Lakshmi opened the door and said: come in, come in! Come inside!
She said: please sit. Then immediately: want coffee? Want tea?
Ananya said: coffee, please. Lakshmi brought hot coffee and spicy snacks.
Then Lakshmi asked: have you eaten? This is not really a question — it's an invitation to eat.
Ananya learned: in Karnataka, you never let a guest leave without offering food. The guest is treated like God.
kn-156: Suresh was not feeling well. He told his wife: I'm not well.
He went to the hospital. He told the doctor: Doctor, I have a headache.
The doctor asked: when did it start? Suresh said: since last night.
The doctor asked: do you have fever? Suresh said: yes, a little fever.
The doctor said: don't worry. I'll write you some medicine. Rest for 3 days.
Suresh said thank you, doctor. He took the medicine and went home to rest.
kn-157: It was a rainy day in Bangalore. Divya looked out the window.
The sky was dark with clouds. The wind was blowing strong.
Divya felt a little sad. She wanted sunshine.
Then she made herself hot coffee. She sat near the window and listened to the rain.
Slowly she felt peaceful. The rain was beautiful. She felt happy.
Sometimes rain brings the best moments, she thought.
kn-158: A tourist stopped Arjun on MG Road. Excuse me, she said. Where is Lalbagh?
Arjun said: from here, go straight. After 2 signals.
Then turn right. A big road will come.
On that road, turn left. Walk for 5 minutes.
You will see the Lalbagh gate. It's nearby, not very far.
The tourist said: thank you very much! Arjun smiled and said: welcome!
kn-159: Kavya called her friend Nisha. Yesterday I worked a lot, she said.
Today I am at home. I am resting.
Nisha asked: what's the plan for tomorrow?
Kavya said: tomorrow I will go to Cubbon Park.
Will you come? Nisha said: yes! I will come!
Kavya said: great! Let's meet at 10 in the morning.
kn-160: Raj moved to Bangalore from Delhi. His colleague invited him for the Kannada New Year celebration.
At the house, the door had a mango leaf decoration. Raj said: very beautiful!
His colleague's mother gave him a mix of neem and jaggery. Neem is bitter. Jaggery is sweet.
She said: life has bitter and sweet. We accept both. This is the meaning of the new year.
They ate a sweet flatbread. Everyone wished each other a happy new year!
Raj felt like he belonged. He said: this is my new home, my new festival.
